tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @exactly_one_set_signbit(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @exactly_one_set_signbit(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XOR_SIGNBITS:%.*]] = xor i8 [[X:%.*]], [[Y:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp slt i8 [[XOR_SIGNBITS]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[R]]
  %xsign = lshr i8 %x, 7
  %ypos = icmp sgt i8 %y, -1
  %yposz = zext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp eq i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

declare void @use(i8)

define i1 @exactly_one_set_signbit_use1(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @exactly_one_set_signbit_use1(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XSIGN:%.*]] = lshr i8 [[X:%.*]], 7
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use(i8 [[XSIGN]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XOR_SIGNBITS:%.*]] = xor i8 [[X]], [[Y:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp slt i8 [[XOR_SIGNBITS]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[R]]
  %xsign = lshr i8 %x, 7
  call void @use(i8 %xsign)
  %ypos = icmp sgt i8 %y, -1
  %yposz = zext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp eq i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define <2 x i1> @same_signbit(<2 x i8> %x, <2 x i8> %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @same_signbit(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XOR_SIGNBITS:%.*]] = xor <2 x i8> [[X:%.*]], [[Y:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp sgt <2 x i8> [[XOR_SIGNBITS]], <i8 -1, i8 -1>
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i1> [[R]]
  %xsign = lshr <2 x i8> %x, <i8 7, i8 7>
  %ypos = icmp sgt <2 x i8> %y, <i8 -1, i8 -1>
  %yposz = zext <2 x i1> %ypos to <2 x i8>
  %r = icmp ne <2 x i8> %xsign, %yposz
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}

; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp sgt <2 x i8> [[XOR_SIGNBITS]], <i8 -1, i8 -1>
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i1> [[R]]
  %xsign = lshr <2 x i8> %x, <i8 7, i8 poison>
  %ypos = icmp sgt <2 x i8> %y, <i8 -1, i8 poison>
  %yposz = zext <2 x i1> %ypos to <2 x i8>
  %r = icmp ne <2 x i8> %xsign, %yposz
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @exactly_one_set_signbit_wrong_shamt(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
  %xsign = lshr i8 %x, 6
  %ypos = icmp sgt i8 %y, -1
  %yposz = zext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp eq i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @exactly_one_set_signbit_wrong_shamt(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
  %xsign = lshr i8 %x, 6
  %ypos = icmp sgt i8 %y, -1
  %yposz = zext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp eq i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define i1 @same_signbit_wrong_type(i8 %x, i32 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @same_signbit_wrong_type(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XSIGN:%.*]] = lshr i8 [[X:%.*]], 7
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[YPOS:%.*]] = icmp sgt i32 [[Y:%.*]], -1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[YPOSZ:%.*]] = zext i1 [[YPOS]] to i8
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = icmp ne i8 [[XSIGN]], [[YPOSZ]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[R]]
  %xsign = lshr i8 %x, 7
  %ypos = icmp sgt i32 %y, -1
  %yposz = zext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp ne i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}

issue.title: [instcombine] cmp no longer optimized
issue.body:
https://godbolt.org/z/zzbMjY1oG
```
bool test(int x, int y) { return (x < 0) == (y >= 0); }
```
ir llvm 14
```
define i1 @test14(i32 %0, i32 %1) {
  %3 = xor i32 %1, %0
  %4 = icmp slt i32 %3, 0
  ret i1 %4
}
```
ir trunk
```
define i1 @test(i32 %0, i32 %1) {
  %3 = lshr i32 %0, 31
  %4 = icmp sgt i32 %1, -1
  %5 = zext i1 %4 to i32
  %6 = icmp eq i32 %3, %5
  ret i1 %6
}
```
issue.author: sftlbcn

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body: We might have changed canonicalization on a sub-pattern, so we need to recognize this check-of-signbits-for-a-pair-of-values pattern.

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
This example should be fixed with:
7cbfc39c77cafe925c0599ab121a67f94bf3870a

There are related patterns that are still not optimized though, so please do file more bugs if you are seeing them. I will try to improve the ones I am aware of either way.
